title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Runtime Contract: Batch Partial Failure
summary: A rename is presented as atomic, replacement loses metadata, delete hides a live version, and concurrent writers silently overwrite each other. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the batch partial failure increment in conditional object-mutation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.
Distinguish unversioned deletion, current-version delete markers, version-specific deletion, retention, legal hold, and recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Batch Partial Failure · Theory Batch Partial Failure
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8576 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Batch Partial Failure contract for the supplied data.
